events.event_description: Donald Richard Norland presented credentials as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim on August 1, 1960, establishing the initial U.S. diplomatic presence in Dahomey prior to the appointment of a resident or nonresident ambassador.

evidence.quote: Donald Richard Norland, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1960-08-01. Note: Presented credentials as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim on July 31, 1960, to take effect on August 1, 1960.

events.event_description: Robert Borden Reams presented credentials as the first Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to Dahomey, serving initially as a nonresident ambassador before Embassy Cotonou was established.

evidence.quote: Robert Borden Reams, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1960-10-14. Credentials presented: 1960-11-26. Superseded 1961-07-31. Note: Commissioned to Dahomey. Commissioned during a recess of the Senate; recommissioned on February 6, 1961, after confirmation. During Reams's tenure as non-resident Ambassador, Embassy Cotonou was established on February 15, 1961, with Converse Hettinger as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

events.event_description: Embassy Cotonou was formally established on February 15, 1961, with Converse Hettinger as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, elevating the U.S. mission from a nonresident ambassadorship to a resident embassy.

evidence.quote: During Reams's tenure as non-resident Ambassador, Embassy Cotonou was established on February 15, 1961, with Converse Hettinger as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.
